title: Retention watermark and expiry surface

backends.replay_boundary_source: in-process replay history watermark when a bounded local window exists
backends.checkpoint_expiry_handling: not durable; subscriber checkpoint expiry is not provider-backed in pure memory mode
backends.checkpoint_cleanup_policy: ephemeral runtime restart clears local history separately from explicit durable checkpoint reset workflows
notes: Provides the baseline replay-boundary vocabulary used by durable backends.
Does not claim provider-backed fail-closed checkpoint expiry.

backends.replay_boundary_source: retention_watermark with oldest/newest retained event IDs and durable trimmed-through sequence
backends.checkpoint_expiry_handling: saved checkpoints fail closed once event_sequence falls behind trimmed_through_sequence
backends.checkpoint_cleanup_policy: history retention advances replay boundaries; checkpoint cleanup remains explicit reset or inactivity-driven cleanup
notes: Persisted retention boundaries survive restart and stay operator-visible.
Expired checkpoints require explicit reset before replay resumes.

backends.replay_boundary_source: remote event-log service watermark mirrored through the HTTP adapter
backends.checkpoint_expiry_handling: remote checkpoints inherit the same fail-closed expiry contract and reset guidance as the durable service backend
backends.checkpoint_cleanup_policy: service-level retention policy is reported independently from subscriber checkpoint reset history and manual cleanup
notes: Proves provider-backed replay boundaries through a repo-native remote service boundary.
Operators can inspect retained oldest/newest boundaries without reading raw backend state.

backends.replay_boundary_source: stub retention floor and event sequence summary captured in checked-in proof artifacts
backends.checkpoint_expiry_handling: stale checkpoints fail closed and require explicit reset to the retained floor proven by BF-07
backends.checkpoint_cleanup_policy: retention-boundary failure is reviewed separately from checkpoint deletion or lease inactivity cleanup
notes: Harness evidence freezes the expiry guidance before a real replicated provider is wired.
Still not treated as the rollout-ready provider-backed adapter.

backends.replay_boundary_source: future provider watermark exposing oldest/newest retained replay boundaries
backends.checkpoint_expiry_handling: future adapter must reject resumes behind the provider retention floor instead of silently fast-forwarding
backends.checkpoint_cleanup_policy: future provider implementation must keep replay retention policy separate from checkpoint cleanup and inactivity TTLs
notes: No real provider-backed retention watermark is wired yet.
Remains contract_only until the first adapter proves persisted boundaries and fail-closed expiry end to end.

policy_split: Retention policy advances oldest/newest retained replay boundaries and is reported through retention_watermark.
Checkpoint cleanup is a separate reset or inactivity-management workflow and must not be conflated with replay compaction.
Expired checkpoints fail closed with reset guidance instead of silently skipping truncated history.
